paper_title: Ornamentation of the Last Foragers and First Herders in the Caspian Region

methods.description: This method involves the systematic recording of the location, color, and microscopic features of shell bead residues, followed by mineralogical composition analysis using Raman Spectroscopy and SEM microprobe analyses. The purpose is to identify the composition and characteristics of residues found on shell beads.

steps.description: The location, color, and microscopic features of the shell bead residues were systematically recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: record location, color, and microscopic features of shell bead residues

steps.description: Their mineralogical composition was subsequently explored with Raman Spectroscopy and SEM microprobe analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze mineralogical composition using Raman Spectroscopy and SEM

steps.description: Raman spectra were obtained on six samples with a SENTERRA confocal microspectrometer (Bruker Optics, Ettlingen, Germany) equipped with a 532 nm excitation line.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain Raman spectra using SENTERRA confocal microspectrometer

steps.description: The spectra, acquired with a 5 mW laser power, 50 co-additions of 5 s excitation, were compared with the RRUFF™ database.
steps.searchable_summary: compare Raman spectra with RRUFF™ database

steps.description: Four samples were characterized using a JEOL JMS 6460 LV scanning electron microscope, operating at an accelerated voltage of 20 kV, coupled with an Oxford X-Max EDS Silicon Drift Detector.
steps.searchable_summary: characterize samples using JEOL JMS 6460 LV SEM and Oxford X-Max EDS

steps.description: Microscopic analysis identifies a red residue concentration within the perforation of the small and midsized shell beads and at the surface of both sides of the large beads.
steps.searchable_summary: identify red residue concentration through microscopic analysis

steps.description: Analysis of the red compound revealed fragments and fine particles of iron oxide, explaining the residue's red coloration.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze red compound for iron oxide content

steps.description: Hematite (Fe2O3) was present as finely grained powder; no particular variation in structure or orientation was visible in the samples.
With regards the ochre particles, analyses identified calcite (CaCO3), gypsum (CaSO₄•2H₂O), quartz (SiO2), and halite (NaCl).
